audios.title: Car Trouble
audios.dialog_text:
A: I think my car is overheating. Can you take a look?

A: About thirty minutes. The temperature light came on.
B: Have you checked the coolant level?
A: I'm not sure how to do that.
B: I'll show you. It's this tank here. It's almost empty.
A: Do I need to add more?
B: Yes, but wait for the engine to cool down first. That's very important.
A: Okay, how long should I wait?
B: At least an hour, to be safe.

audios.title: Gym Membership
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm interested in joining your gym. What are the rates?

A: What's included in the membership?
B: You get access to all equipment, group classes, and the pool.
A: Are there any extra fees?
B: There's a one-time registration fee of $25.
A: Do you offer personal training?
B: Yes, but that's an additional cost. It's $40 per session.
A: Okay, I'll think about it. Can I try a free class first?

A: David Miller.
B: Please wait here. I'll be right back.

audios.title: Online Shopping
audios.dialog_text:

A: It's too small. I ordered a large, but this feels like a medium.
B: Did you check the size chart before ordering?

B: What's the return policy?
A: They offer free returns within thirty days.
B: That's good. Are you going to exchange it for a larger size?

audios.title: Advice from a Friend
audios.dialog_text:
A: I don't know what to do about my job. I'm really unhappy there.
B: Have you thought about looking for something else?
A: I have, but I'm scared to make a change.
B: What's the worst that could happen?
A: I might end up in a worse situation.
B: Or you could end up much happier. You'll never know unless you try.
A: That's true. I've been there for five years with no promotion.
B: It sounds like you've already made up your mind.
A: I guess I have. I'm going to update my resume tonight.
B: Good for you! I'm proud of you for taking action.

audios.title: Financial Planning
audios.dialog_text:
A: I want to start saving money, but I don't know where to begin.
B: The first step is to track your spending for a month.
A: That sounds like a lot of work.
B: It's actually not that hard. There are apps that do it for you.
A: Okay, I can try that. Then what?
B: Once you know where your money is going, you can create a budget.
A: How much should I try to save each month?
B: A good rule of thumb is to save 20% of your income.

B: Start with 10% and work your way up. Every little bit helps.

A: I've been running three times a week, but I'm not seeing results.

B: Do you track your calories? You might be eating more than you realize.

B: There are apps that make it really easy. Just scan the barcode.

audios.title: Buying a Car
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm thinking about buying my first car. Do you have any advice?
B: First, set a budget and stick to it. Don't let the dealer upsell you.
A: What about buying used versus new?
B: Used is usually better for your first car. You won't lose as much value.
A: How do I know if I'm getting a good deal?
B: Research online before you go. Look at similar cars in the area.
A: Should I bring someone with me?
B: Definitely. Bring a friend who knows about cars. Two heads are better than one.
A: What about financing? Should I get a loan from the dealer?
B: Compare rates from your bank first. Often they offer better terms.

audios.title: Party Planning

A: Next Saturday, and I'm so behind on planning.
B: First things first, what's the guest list like?
A: About thirty people, mostly his coworkers and our friends.
B: Are you having it at your house or somewhere else?

B: What about renting a room at a restaurant?

audios.title: Pet Adoption
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm thinking about adopting a dog, but I've never had one before.
B: That's a big responsibility. Have you done any research?

A: How much does that usually cost per month?
B: About $100 for food, plus maybe $50 for other supplies.

B: For a first-time owner, I'd suggest a medium-sized, calm breed.

audios.title: Cooking Disaster

A: I burned the chicken, and the rice was still hard in the middle.

A: I tried to, but I got distracted and forgot to set a timer.
B: We've all been there. What did you end up eating?
A: We ordered pizza. She was nice about it, but I felt terrible.
B: Don't be so hard on yourself. Cooking takes practice.

audios.title: Teaching Kids Responsibility
audios.dialog_text:
A: How do you get your kids to do their chores without complaining?
B: I started giving them an allowance. It really motivates them.
A: How much do you give them?
B: $5 per week for basic chores like taking out the trash and making their beds.
A: Do they ever refuse to do them?
B: Sometimes, but then they don't get paid. They learn quickly.
A: What about bigger tasks like cleaning the garage?
B: Those are extra. I pay them $10 for big jobs like that.
A: I should try that system. My kids just ignore me when I ask.
B: Make sure you're consistent. That's the key.

audios.title: Learning Guitar
audios.dialog_text:
A: I bought a guitar last month, but I'm not making much progress.
B: Are you taking lessons or trying to learn on your own?
A: I've been using YouTube videos, but it's not the same as a real teacher.
B: Have you considered an app? There are some really good ones.
A: I tried a few, but I got bored after a couple of weeks.
B: Maybe you need to set a specific goal, like learning one song.
A: That's a good idea. What song would you recommend for a beginner?

audios.title: Neighbor Dispute
audios.dialog_text:
A: I have a problem with my neighbor. He plays loud music late at night.
B: Have you tried talking to him about it?

B: Maybe you should knock and talk to him face to face.
A: I'm not very confrontational. What if he gets angry?

B: Then you can file a noise complaint with the building manager.

audios.title: Job Promotion

A: I just got offered a promotion at work!

B: Does it come with a raise?
A: Yes, a 15% increase, plus a company car.
B: That sounds like a great package. Are you going to take it?

B: You've been preparing for this for years. You'll do great.

audios.title: Wedding Planning

A: The guest list. My parents want to invite everyone they know.

A: Over 200. We only wanted around 100.
B: You need to set a firm number and stick to it.

audios.title: Technology Addiction
audios.dialog_text:
A: I think I spend way too much time on my phone. It's becoming a problem.
B: How many hours a day would you say you're on it?
A: Probably five or six hours. Maybe more on weekends.
B: That is a lot. What are you usually doing on it?
A: Social media mostly, scrolling through feeds mindlessly.
B: Have you tried setting screen time limits on your phone?
A: I have, but I always ignore them. It's too easy to override.
B: What about leaving your phone in another room when you're home?
A: That makes me anxious. I feel like I'm missing something.
B: That's exactly the addiction talking. Try it for one hour and see how you feel.

audios.title: Volunteering
audios.dialog_text:
A: I've been thinking about volunteering on weekends. Any suggestions?
B: What causes are you passionate about?
A: I love animals. Maybe an animal shelter?
B: That's a great choice. Shelters always need help with walking dogs.
A: Do I need any special training for that?
B: Usually just a short orientation. They'll show you how to handle the dogs.
A: How many hours should I commit to?
B: Most places are happy with as little as two hours per week.
A: I can definitely do that. Do I just show up and ask?
B: Call ahead and ask about their volunteer application process.

audios.title: Starting a Business
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm thinking about starting my own bakery. What do you think?
B: You make the best cookies I've ever had. I think it's a great idea.
A: Thanks! I'm just nervous about the financial risk.
B: Have you written a business plan yet?
A: Not yet. I don't even know where to start.
B: There are templates online. You need to estimate your costs.
A: What are the biggest expenses?
B: Rent for the space, equipment, ingredients, and labor.
A: I might start small, selling at farmers' markets first.
B: That's smart. You can test the market without too much risk.

questions.question: What business does the person want to start?
What does the friend suggest doing first?
Where might the person start selling first?

audios.title: Bad Haircut
audios.dialog_text:
A: I just got a haircut, and I hate it. What should I do?
B: Let me see... Oh, it's not that bad. Maybe a little short.
A: A little? She cut off three inches more than I asked for!
B: Did you tell her you didn't like it while you were there?
A: I was too shy to say anything. I just paid and left.
B: You should call and explain. Maybe they can fix it for free.
A: I don't want to go back. I'm too embarrassed.
B: Then just let it grow out. In a month, it'll look better.
A: You're right. I'm overreacting. It's just hair. It'll grow.

questions.question: How much more was cut than requested?
Why didn't the person speak up at the salon?
What does the friend suggest doing?

audios.title: Public Speaking
audios.dialog_text:
A: I have to give a presentation at work tomorrow, and I'm terrified.
B: Public speaking is scary, but preparation makes a huge difference.
A: I've prepared, but I'm still so nervous.
B: Practice in front of a mirror or record yourself.
A: I've done that a few times, but it's not the same as real people.
B: Practice in front of a friend or family member. Get used to eyes on you.
A: That's a good idea. What about my voice? I sound so shaky.
B: Take deep breaths before you start. It'll calm your nerves.
A: What if I forget what I'm supposed to say?
B: Keep notecards with bullet points, not full sentences.

questions.question: What is the person scared of?
What does the friend suggest practicing?
What should the person put on notecards?

audios.title: Apartment Noise
audios.dialog_text:
A: The upstairs neighbor's kids run around all day. It's driving me crazy.
B: Have you talked to the neighbor about it?
A: I knocked once, but no one answered.
B: Try leaving a polite note explaining the situation.
A: I'm worried they'll get defensive and make it worse.
B: Most people don't realize how much noise travels through floors.
A: That's true. They might not even know it's a problem.
B: If that doesn't work, talk to the building manager.
A: I hope it doesn't come to that. I'll try a note first.
B: Be friendly and suggest they put down rugs to muffle the sound.

questions.question: What is the problem with the upstairs neighbor?
What did the person try first?
What does the friend suggest using to muffle sound?

audios.title: Late Night Work
audios.dialog_text:
A: I've been working until midnight every day this week. I'm exhausted.
B: That's not sustainable. Is it always this busy?
A: No, it's just a project deadline. It should get better next week.
B: Make sure you're taking care of yourself. Sleep is important.
A: I know, but I can't fall behind. There's too much at stake.
B: Can you delegate any of your tasks to others?
A: Everyone else is just as busy as I am.
B: What about working from home a couple of days to save commute time?
A: That's a good idea. I'll ask my boss about it tomorrow.
B: And don't forget to eat properly. Junk food won't help your energy.

questions.question: Why is the person working so late?
What does the friend suggest to save time?
What does the friend say about food?

audios.title: Friendship Problems
audios.dialog_text:
A: My best friend hasn't talked to me in two weeks, and I don't know why.
B: Did you have an argument that I don't know about?
A: No, everything seemed fine. Then suddenly she stopped responding.
B: Have you tried reaching out again?
A: I sent a text yesterday, but she left me on read.
B: Maybe something is going on in her life that she hasn't shared.
A: I didn't think about that. Maybe she's dealing with something hard.
B: Give her space for a few more days, then try calling instead of texting.
A: You're right. A phone call is more personal.
B: If she still doesn't respond, then you might need to accept that the friendship is changing.

questions.question: How long has the friend not talked to the person?
What did the person try yesterday?
What does the friend suggest trying next?

audios.title: Buying a House
audios.dialog_text:
A: We found a house we love, but it's at the top of our budget.
B: How much over your ideal price is it?
A: About $30,000 more than we wanted to spend.
B: That's significant, but not impossible. What's included?
A: It has a finished basement and a huge backyard.
B: Those are features you'd pay for anyway if you bought a cheaper house.
A: That's what we're thinking. It might be worth it in the long run.
B: Have you factored in property taxes and maintenance costs?
A: Yes, we calculated everything. We can afford it, but it'll be tight.
B: Only you can decide if the stretch is worth it. Trust your gut.

questions.question: How much more is the house than the budget?
What features does the house have?
What advice does the friend give?

audios.title: Learning a Language
audios.dialog_text:
A: I've been studying French for six months, and I still can't hold a conversation.
B: Language learning takes time. Are you practicing speaking every day?
A: Not every day. I mostly use an app to learn vocabulary.
B: Apps are good for vocabulary, but not for speaking.
A: I'm too nervous to speak with real people.
B: There are apps where you can practice with AI first to build confidence.
A: That might help. Do you have any recommendations?
B: Try HelloTalk. You can chat with native speakers learning your language.
A: That sounds perfect. I'll download it today.

questions.question: How long has the person been studying French?
What does the person mostly use to learn?
What app does the friend recommend?
